message:
chore(beads): add acceptance_criteria to QA coverage uplift sub-beads

Enrich three QA-COVERAGE child beads (bd-1f42.8.4.1, bd-1f42.8.4.2,
bd-1f42.8.4.3) with explicit structured acceptance_criteria fields
detailing rubric floor compliance, coverage delta documentation, and
residual risk cataloging requirements for each module surface area.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -141,14 +141,14 @@
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.2.3","title":"[QA-NONMOCK] Audit extension-related allowlist exceptions for expiry and removal","description":"Review extension-related allowlisted doubles in docs/testing-policy.md, enforce owner+expiry+replacement-plan completeness, and remove expired or unjustified entries. Acceptance: exception table is current, time-boxed, and aligned with actual test usage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All allowlist exceptions include owner, expiry, replacement plan, and linked bead.\n2. Expired/unjustified entries are removed or renewed with explicit rationale.\n3. Policy table matches actual in-repo usage from latest inventory scan.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:43:54.212344218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:40.919691225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:43:54.212344218Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:42.533524862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:42.893121141Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":988,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Use docs/testing-policy.md allowlist table as source of truth. Every retained exception must have owner, expires_at, replacement_plan, and narrow scope; remove stale entries.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.3","title":"[QA-NONMOCK] Burn down remaining unit-inline doubles outside critical extension clusters","description":"Task:
Systematically reduce residual Mock/Fake/Stub/Dummy/Null patterns across unit-inline and unit suites outside the extension-dispatcher hotspot.

Scope examples:
- src/bin inline tests using mock-like helpers
- tests/model_selector_cycling and other unit files with stub dependencies
- any newly discovered unclassified helper doubles that leak into suite.unit semantics

Acceptance checks:
- Unit and unit-inline inventories show downward trend in entry_count and high-risk totals.
- Suite.unit remains free from disallowed doubles per docs/testing-policy.md policy.
- Exception inventory is explicit, justified, and expiry-bound.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Residual non-extension unit/unit-inline doubles are reduced and summarized by module.\n2. suite.unit remains compliant with testing policy (no disallowed doubles).\n3. Remaining exceptions are explicitly time-boxed and auditable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:42.909488998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:27.532995420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:42.909488998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:41.457637829Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":978,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Evidence anchor:
- Inventory also shows residual doubles outside top extension clusters (including unit/unit-inline and helper leakage risk).

Scope rule:
This task intentionally excludes the main extension hotspot (handled in bd-1f42.8.2) and focuses on all remaining residuals to avoid hidden backlog drift.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:46:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.4","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Raise critical-module non-mock coverage to rubric floors and targets","description":"Task:
Increase non-mock test coverage depth on critical modules using real execution paths and policy-compliant fixtures.

Critical surfaces:
- src/agent.rs
- src/tools.rs
- src/providers/*.rs + src/provider.rs
- src/session.rs and session index/persistence surfaces
- src/extensions.rs / src/extensions_js.rs risk pathways

Acceptance checks:
- Coverage meets or exceeds module floors in docs/non-mock-rubric.json.
- Upward trend toward module targets is demonstrated in refreshed coverage-baseline-map artifacts.
- Any module below target has explicit follow-up beads and rationale.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Critical-module non-mock coverage meets rubric floors in docs/non-mock-rubric.json.\n2. Coverage-baseline map shows upward movement toward targets for all critical surfaces.\n3. Any module still below target has explicit follow-up bead and owner.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:50.123328955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:31.615911214Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:50.123328955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:43.277408504Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:43.982840426Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":979,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage anchor:
- docs/non-mock-rubric.json defines module floors/targets.
- docs/coverage-baseline-map.json shows current baseline is materially below full coverage and includes explicit uncovered counts by critical path.

Dependency note:
Should consume outputs from mock-burn-down tasks first so coverage gains reflect real-path tests, not synthetic inflation.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:46:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.1","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Uplift non-mock coverage for agent/tools orchestration paths","description":"Add deterministic non-mock tests for abort/retry/interrupt/tool-iteration and tool error/timeout edges across src/agent.rs and src/tools.rs. Acceptance: floor compliance in rubric with explicit before/after deltas.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:05.128598418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:02.580089287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:44.259522782Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:05.128598418Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":989,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage emphasis: abort/retry/interrupt control flow in src/agent.rs and timeout/process-tree cleanup/error paths in src/tools.rs under non-mock execution.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.2","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Uplift non-mock coverage for providers/session surfaces","description":"Expand provider routing/stream normalization and session persistence/replay tests using real-path harnesses and deterministic fixtures (not unit stubs). Acceptance: provider/session modules at or above rubric floors with documented residual risks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:12.964119968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:02.920966749Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:44.537952312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:12.964119968Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":990,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage emphasis: provider routing/stream event normalization plus session persistence/index/replay drift paths under deterministic integration conditions.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Uplift non-mock coverage for extensions/auth/error critical paths","description":"Target uncovered extension-runtime, auth-redaction, and error-hint paths with deterministic integration coverage and explicit edge-case assertions. Acceptance: documented coverage gains and no policy regressions in sensitive error/auth handling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:23.477301938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:41.646453191Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:44.816159598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:23.477301938Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":991,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage emphasis: extension runtime edge cases, auth redaction boundaries, and error-hint fidelity. Assert no leakage of secrets and no regression in operator diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.4","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Uplift non-mock coverage for CLI/config/resources/models/rpc/tui surfaces","description":"Add deterministic non-mock unit/integration coverage for secondary-but-critical user-facing surfaces not fully captured in current sub-beads: CLI arg parsing/dispatch, config loading/merge precedence, resource loading, model registry resolution, RPC/stdin protocol handling, and TUI rendering state transitions. Acceptance: each surface has explicit edge-case tests and documented coverage deltas in the refreshed baseline map.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:36.152524691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:36.152524691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:36.152524691Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Add branch-focused edge/failure-path tests for critical non-mock modules","description":"Design and implement branch-focused deterministic tests for negative/error pathways (timeouts, malformed payloads, recovery fallbacks, cancellation edges, auth/error hint formatting) across critical modules already in scope. This bead ensures apparent line coverage is backed by meaningful branch/assertion depth. Acceptance: critical branch paths are explicitly enumerated, tested, and reflected in updated coverage artifacts when branch export is available.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:40.745671897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.811561822Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:40.745671897Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:07.918478185Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.202857468Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.497979943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.811495699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Acceptance: documented coverage gains and no policy regressions in sensitive error/auth handling.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Extension/auth/error critical paths have deterministic non-mock edge-case tests.\n2. Redaction and user-facing error-hint behavior is validated for sensitive failures.\n3. Coverage deltas demonstrate measurable improvement.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:23.477301938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:04.225920510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:44.816159598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:23.477301938Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":991,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage emphasis: extension runtime edge cases, auth redaction boundaries, and error-hint fidelity. Assert no leakage of secrets and no regression in operator diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.4","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Uplift non-mock coverage for CLI/config/resources/models/rpc/tui surfaces","description":"Add deterministic non-mock unit/integration coverage for secondary-but-critical user-facing surfaces not fully captured in current sub-beads: CLI arg parsing/dispatch, config loading/merge precedence, resource loading, model registry resolution, RPC/stdin protocol handling, and TUI rendering state transitions. Acceptance: each surface has explicit edge-case tests and documented coverage deltas in the refreshed baseline map.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CLI/config/resources/models/rpc/tui surfaces each have explicit edge-case non-mock tests.\n2. Tests cover user-visible correctness invariants (dispatch precedence, config merge, registry resolution, protocol correctness, render state).\n3. Coverage delta for these surfaces is recorded in refreshed baseline artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:36.152524691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:09.393106747Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:36.152524691Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","title":"[QA-COVERAGE] Add branch-focused edge/failure-path tests for critical non-mock modules","description":"Design and implement branch-focused deterministic tests for negative/error pathways (timeouts, malformed payloads, recovery fallbacks, cancellation edges, auth/error hint formatting) across critical modules already in scope. This bead ensures apparent line coverage is backed by meaningful branch/assertion depth. Acceptance: critical branch paths are explicitly enumerated, tested, and reflected in updated coverage artifacts when branch export is available.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Branch-focused negative/error path matrix is defined and linked to concrete tests.\n2. Critical failure branches are covered with strong assertions, not line-only coverage.\n3. Branch/line/function evidence is updated (branch where exporter is stable).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:40.745671897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:12.519868678Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:09:40.745671897Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:07.918478185Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.202857468Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.497979943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:11:08.811495699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f42.8.5","title":"[QA-E2E] Complete scenario matrix for end-to-end integration scripts","description":"Task:
Produce and enforce an explicit E2E scenario completeness matrix covering success, failure, recovery, retry, interruption, and multi-provider parity paths.

Expected outputs:
- Matrix artifact mapping workflow -> script/test -> provider family -> expected evidence.
- Missing scenario scripts/tests added with deterministic setup and teardown.
- Explicit skip rationale for intentionally unsupported live paths.

Acceptance checks:
- Every matrix row is backed by a concrete executable test or documented waiver.
- run_all profile outputs reference matrix coverage in summary/evidence metadata.
- No high-risk workflow remains unowned/unmapped.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Canonical scenario matrix exists and each row maps to executable script/test or approved waiver.\n2. High-risk workflow classes (success, failure, recovery, retry, interruption) are represented with deterministic assertions.\n3. run_all artifacts reference matrix coverage and unresolved rows fail gating.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:57.301219288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:34.454480611Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.378146026Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:42:57.301219288Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.092206390Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":980,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E anchor:
- tests/suite_classification.toml enumerates many e2e suites, but no single matrix currently proves complete workflow-to-scenario mapping with waiver accounting.

Dependency note:
Linked to bd-1f42.3.5 because soak/stability scenarios and logging depth are part of completion criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:46:38Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","title":"[QA-E2E] Generate canonical scenario-to-test coverage matrix artifact","description":"Build and version a canonical matrix mapping critical workflows to concrete E2E tests/scripts, provider families, and required artifacts. Acceptance: matrix is machine-readable, CI-consumed, and diffed for drift.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:33.317309217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03.614875659Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.660464589Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:33.317309217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":992,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Matrix should map: workflow -> suite/test -> provider family -> expected artifacts -> replay command. Store machine-readable artifact for CI diffing.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","title":"[QA-E2E] Implement missing high-risk workflow scenarios","description":"For every uncovered high-risk row in the scenario matrix, add deterministic E2E scripts/tests with pass/fail assertions and artifact outputs. Acceptance: no high-risk workflow remains without executable coverage or approved waiver.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:44.140403343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03.958600845Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:44:44.140403343Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:45:45.946478894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":993,"issue_id":"bd-1f42.8.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implement scenarios for each uncovered high-risk matrix row; include positive and failure-path assertions with deterministic setup/teardown and artifact capture.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:03Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1f42.8.5.1","title":"[QA-E2E] Generate canonical scenario-to-test coverage matrix artifact","description":"Build and version a canonical matrix mapping critical workflows to concrete E2E tests/scripts, provider families, and required artifacts.  {"id":"bd-9sa","title":"Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper","description":"# Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper

## Goal
Create a functional HTTP client in src/http/client.rs using asupersync APIs.

## Current State
src/http/client.rs has:
- Client struct (empty)
- RequestBuilder struct with method/url/headers/body
- RequestBuilder::send() returns Err("asupersync HTTP client not yet implemented")
- RequestBuilder::json() silently ignores serialization errors

## Implementation Requirements

### Client API
```rust
use asupersync::{Cx, http::client::HttpClient};
use asupersync::tls::TlsConnectorBuilder;

pub struct Client {
    inner: HttpClient,
}

impl Client {
    pub fn new() -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Create TLS connector with native roots
        let tls = TlsConnectorBuilder::new()
            .with_native_roots()?
            .build()?;
        let inner = HttpClient::new(tls);
        Ok(Self { inner })
    }
    
    pub fn request(&self, method: Method, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder {
        RequestBuilder::new(self, method, url)
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder { ... }
    pub fn post(&self, url: &str) -> RequestBuilder { ... }
}
```

### RequestBuilder API
```rust
impl RequestBuilder {
    pub fn header(mut self, key: &str, value: &str) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn json<T: Serialize>(mut self, body: &T) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn body(mut self, bytes: Vec<u8>) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn timeout(mut self, duration: Duration) -> Self { ... }
    
    pub async fn send(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Response, Error> {
        // Actually send the request using asupersync
    }
}
```

### Response API
```rust
pub struct Response {
    status: StatusCode,
    headers: Headers,
    body: BodyReader,  // Async streaming body
}

impl Response {
    pub fn status(&self) -> StatusCode { ... }
    pub fn headers(&self) -> &Headers { ... }
    pub async fn text(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<String, Error> { ... }
    pub async fn json<T: DeserializeOwned>(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<T, Error> { ... }
    pub fn bytes_stream(self) -> impl Stream<Item = Result<Bytes, Error>> { ... }
}
```

## Key Considerations
1. **TLS**: Use rustls with native root certificates (no openssl)
2. **Timeout**: Support per-request timeout via asupersync Budget
3. **Streaming**: Response body must support async streaming for SSE
4. **Headers**: Support standard headers (Content-Type, Authorization, etc.)
5. **Error handling**: Wrap asupersync errors in pi's Error type

## Testing
- Unit test: Client creation succeeds
- Unit test: RequestBuilder builds correct request
- Integration test: GET request to httpbin.org/get
- Integration test: POST with JSON body
- Integration test: Timeout behavior

## Files
- src/http/client.rs (modify)
- src/http/mod.rs (update re-exports)
- src/error.rs (add HTTP error variants)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Client::new() creates working HTTP client
- [ ] GET/POST requests work
- [ ] JSON body serialization works
- [ ] Response streaming works
- [ ] Timeout respected
- [ ] TLS verification works
- [ ] All unit tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:01.120130883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:57.792527506Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:12:08.576706438Z","close_reason":"Completed (HTTP client wrapper is implemented + tests pass; see comments)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9sa","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":131,"issue_id":"bd-9sa","author":"AmberForest","text":"Taking this bead: implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper (no tokio/reqwest). Will wire streaming + TLS + timeout per spec.","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:45:33Z"},{"id":132,"issue_id":"bd-9sa","author":"AmberForest","text":"Implemented asupersync HTTP wrapper updates (Cx-aware send + timeout + StatusCode + bytes_stream). Migrated auth + npm registry calls to wrapper with Cx::for_request. Your bd-9sa implementation in src/http/client.rs looks already landed; after I run full quality gates I may propose closing bd-9sa to unblock provider-migration beads.","created_at":"2026-02-03T16:58:01Z"},{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-9sa","author":"CodexGPT52","text":"Ran full quality gates on main workspace: cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, cargo test (all pass). src/http/client.rs is a working streaming HTTP+TLS client (providers/tests exercise it).  {"id":"bd-ah1","title":"Implement session persistence cycle tests","description":"# Implement session persistence cycle tests

## Goal
Test the full session lifecycle: create → save → reload → verify integrity, with detailed logs.

## Background
Session persistence involves:
- JSONL file format (one JSON object per line)
- Header with metadata (version, cwd, timestamp)
- Message entries with parent/child relationships
- Tree structure for conversation branching
- File locking for concurrent access

Current tests cover serialization but not full I/O cycles.

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- Use temp dirs and deterministic paths; avoid asserting exact timestamps.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log session path, header fields, message IDs, tree shape, and file sizes.
- On failure, dump the JSONL contents (redacted) and parsed header.

## Test Categories

### 1. Basic Persistence Cycle Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_session_save_and_reload() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project/path");
    session.add_user_message("Hello");
    session.add_assistant_message("Hi there!");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.messages().len(), 2);
}
```

### 2. Tree Structure Persistence Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_branching_persists() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/project");
    let root_id = session.add_user_message("Root");
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch A");

    session.navigate_to(root_id.clone());
    session.add_assistant_message("Branch B");

    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    let children = loaded.children_of(&root_id);
    assert_eq!(children.len(), 2);
}
```

### 3. Header Metadata Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_header_metadata_preserved() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let mut session = Session::new("/my/project/path");
    session.set_provider("anthropic");
    session.set_model("claude-opus-4");
    session.set_thinking_level(ThinkingLevel::High);
    session.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let loaded = Session::load(&session_path).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(loaded.cwd(), "/my/project/path");
}
```

### 4. Entry Type Persistence Tests
- ModelChange entries
- ThinkingLevel entries
- Compaction entries
- Tool invocation entries (if serialized)

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Missing file
- Corrupted JSONL
- Partial corruption (valid header + bad line)

### 6. Concurrent Access Tests
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_concurrent_writes_locked() {
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let session_path = temp.path().join("test.jsonl");

    let session1 = Session::new("/project");
    session1.persist(&session_path).await.unwrap();

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle1 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 1");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let path = session_path.clone();
    let handle2 = spawn(async move {
        let mut s = Session::load(&path).await.unwrap();
        s.add_user_message("From task 2");
        s.persist(&path).await
    });

    let (r1, r2) = join(handle1, handle2);
    assert!(r1.unwrap().is_ok() || r2.unwrap().is_ok());
}
```

### 7. Directory Structure Tests
- Persist creates nested directories when needed

## Dependencies
None (uses existing session module)

## Files
- tests/session_persistence.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ session persistence tests
- [ ] Save/reload cycle tested
- [ ] Tree structure preserved
- [ ] All entry types tested
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Concurrent access tested
- [ ] Logs include paths + header fields
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"claude-opus","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:00.745045518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:26:08.213957493Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:49:20.977021223Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ah1","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ah1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":187,"issue_id":"bd-ah1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session persistence cycle tests implementation complete.

**7 new tests added to tests/session_conformance.rs:**

1. save_preserves_header_provider_and_model
   - Tests provider, model_id, and thinking_level header fields survive save/reload

2. save_preserves_header_cwd
   - Tests cwd (working directory) header field preservation

3. save_round_trips_tool_result_message
   - Tests ToolResult messages with tool_call_id, tool_name, content

4. save_round_trips_tool_error_result
   - Tests ToolResult with is_error=true flag preservation

5. save_preserves_unicode_message_content
   - Tests CJK, Hebrew, Arabic, and emoji content preservation

6. save_preserves_emoji_in_assistant_response
   - Tests emoji preservation in assistant response content blocks

7. save_preserves_message_timestamps
   - Tests message timestamp field preservation

**Pre-existing tests already covered:**
- Basic persistence cycle (linear messages)
- Tree structure persistence (branching)
- Most entry types (ModelChange, ThinkingLevel, Compaction, Label, BranchSummary, Custom)
- Error handling (missing file, empty, corrupted)
- Concurrent access safety
- Directory structure creation

Total: 37 tests passing, comprehensive session persistence coverage.

+{"id":"bd-anewk","title":"[PERF-4] Frame budget enforcement with degraded rendering modes","description":"## Problem

When the system is under load, the TUI continues trying to render at full fidelity 60fps, which can make the terminal unresponsive. Need automatic degradation.

## Solution: Frame Budget State Machine

### State Machine

```
Normal (60fps, full markdown)
  ↓ 3 consecutive frames > 16ms
Degraded (30fps, simplified markdown)
  ↓ 3 consecutive frames > 33ms
Emergency (15fps, raw text, no markdown)
  ↑ 10 consecutive frames < budget → recovery
```

### Degradation Levels

1. **Normal**: Full markdown rendering, 60fps, all visual features
2. **Degraded**: Simplified markdown (no syntax highlighting in code blocks, simpler tables), 30fps
   - Skip glamour rendering, use lightweight markdown (bold/italic only)
   - Reduce viewport content refresh rate (every 2nd frame)
3. **Emergency**: Raw text (no markdown at all), 15fps
   - Display assistant messages as plain text
   - Skip thinking block rendering entirely
   - Minimal chrome (no spinner animation, static status)

### Implementation

```rust
enum RenderFidelity { Normal, Degraded, Emergency }

struct FrameBudget {
    fidelity: RenderFidelity,
    consecutive_over: u8,   // frames exceeding budget
    consecutive_under: u8,  // frames under budget (for recovery)
    budget_us: u64,         // current budget in microseconds
}
```

### Recovery with Hysteresis
- Degraded → Normal: 10 consecutive frames under 12ms (not 16ms — 25% margin)
- Emergency → Degraded: 10 consecutive frames under 25ms
- This prevents oscillation between modes

### FPS Adjustment
- Communicate desired FPS to bubbletea runtime
- charmed_rust ProgramOptions.fps is set at startup but could be patched via Cmd
- Alternative: skip rendering on even frames when in Degraded mode (effective 30fps)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: FrameBudget struct, integration into view()/update()

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-3 (frame timing telemetry — need measurements to enforce budgets)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameBudget state machine with 3 levels
- [ ] Hysteresis prevents oscillation
- [ ] Degraded mode skips expensive markdown rendering
- [ ] Emergency mode renders raw text only
- [ ] Recovery path back to Normal after sustained good performance
- [ ] Unit tests for state machine transitions
- [ ] E2E test with synthetic load showing degradation → recovery cycle","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:07.564506084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:07.564506084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-axuu","title":"Workstream: /settings UI Parity + config persistence","description":"# Goal
Bring `/settings` and config editing UX to parity with legacy pi-mono:
- `/settings` opens an in-app UI for common options.
- Changes persist to JSON settings files with correct precedence (global vs project).

# Legacy Spec
- Settings docs: `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md`
- `/settings` is listed in interactive command table in legacy README.

Legacy `/settings` covers (at least):
- thinking level
- theme
- message delivery (steering/follow-up)
- model cycling scope

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `/settings` prints a JSON summary only; no editor UI.
- Several config fields exist in `src/config.rs` but are not wired into the interactive UX.

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Implement a `/settings` UI component:
   - select/toggle settings
   - show current value
   - confirm/apply
2) Persist changes:
   - write to `~/.pi/agent/settings.json` (global) and/or `.pi/settings.json` (project)
   - follow existing merge semantics from `src/config.rs`
3) Ensure changes take effect without restart where possible.

# Notes
- Avoid editing raw JSON in the TUI for now; prefer focused selectors.
- For settings that depend on other workstreams (themes, keybindings), include partial functionality but keep UX consistent.

# Testing
- Unit tests for persistence and merge semantics.
- State tests for `/settings` selection flows.

# Dependencies
- Keybinding workstream (`bd-3ip`) for consistent navigation.
- Theme system workstream (`bd-22p`) for actual theme switching.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] /settings opens interactive UI and restores editor
[ ] Changes persist to global/project settings
[ ] Message delivery settings configurable
[ ] Theme selection supported (once theme system exists)
[ ] Display/editor toggles configurable
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] /settings opens interactive UI and restores editor
[ ] Changes persist to global/project settings
[ ] Message delivery settings configurable
[ ] Theme selection supported (once theme system exists)
[ ] Display/editor toggles configurable
[ ] Unit tests and integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] Integration/E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows and failure modes; emit detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test

 {"id":"bd-aymk","title":"Coverage: raise CI gate to 40% (no-mock)","description":"# Goal\nRaise CI line coverage gate from 30% to 40% while keeping the project’s no-mock policy.\n\n# Scope / Deliverables\n- Add real-path unit/integration tests (no mock providers; VCR playback only when network would otherwise be required).\n- Prefer low-risk pure-logic hotspots first (parsing/normalization/enum conversions/cost math) to gain coverage without destabilizing runtime code.\n- Once running: cargo llvm-cov --all-targets --workspace --summary-only reports >= 40% lines, update GitHub CI to enforce 40% (set fail-under-lines to 40).\n- Update docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md baseline and list the new coverage contributors.\n\n# Suggested Coverage Targets (low-risk first)\n- src/provider.rs: Api/KnownProvider parsing + Display + Model::calculate_cost invariants\n- src/http/client.rs: URL redaction + request building + error mapping invariants\n- src/lib.rs: public API smoke + lintable invariants\n\n# Constraints\n- No mocking crates (CI guard already enforces).\n- Deterministic tests; no network in CI (VCR playback only).\n\n# Acceptance Criteria\n- CI coverage gate is 40% and green.\n- No-mock policy remains enforced.\n- Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.\n","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:28:58.414134200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:11:09.197774773Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:11:09.197627278Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aymk","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:28:58.626024502Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-aymk","depends_on_id":"bd-doi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:28:58.689836244Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
